title: Experienced Game Designer - Victoria 3
description:
#### ***Are you a creative and dedicated games industry professional with experience in game design and a passion for strategy games? Are you interested in Paradox Grand Strategy Games and eager to help us take them to the next level? Then you may be the one we’re looking for!***

**About the Role**
------------------


As a Game Designer working with the Paradox Development Studio in Stockholm, you will be an on-site project team member involved in designing gameplay features and systems under the guidance of the Game Director. You will have a direct and influential impact on the quality and feel of the game.


We are seeking new employees who enjoy working in a team where everyone supports each other, but where you are also given the freedom and responsibility to take charge of a task and deliver. We care about quality and are motivated by the idea of our many fans around the world praising your work after release. You will thrive in a modern, creative environment where people working with you love their jobs and the games we make.

**Relationships**
-----------------

* The Game Designer will have the Operations Manager as their direct manager while collaborating closely with the Design Lead and Game Director on day-to-day tasks, priorities, and design direction
* Collaboration with a multidisciplinary team (other designers, programmers, artists, production, and QA) to lead task breakdown and estimation, ensure quality through continuous playtesting and iteration, and optimize design, system efficiency, and content quality
* Engagement on various levels to identify and solve design problems, e.g., fanbase, other Paradox designers and Game Directors, user research, and analytics team

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

* Design and implement new game systems and features
* Help to deliver the creative vision set by the Game Director
* Write and maintain design documentation
* Come up with your own ideas and creative contributions
* Work closely with other members of the team to make sure that implementation is done according to the design
* Be autonomous and proactive in your work
* Coaching junior designers and providing constructive feedback on their work
* Delegation of less complex tasks to junior designers
* Uphold good relationships within the team with a clear understanding of expectations for the game we are making

**Required Qualifications**
---------------------------

* At least three years of professional games industry experience in Game Design
* A demonstrable experience with complex system and feature designs
* Ability to work highly independently with assigned tasks and take responsibility for delivering game design specifications according to the project’s schedule
* A solid understanding of core loops and player experience
* A genuine passion for strategy games and Paradox games in particular
* Fluent in spoken and written English
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills to work and inspire in a team environment

**As a bonus, you might have**
------------------------------

* An academic or personal interest in history, economics, and/or politics
* Experience with programming or scripting
* Experience with creating mods for Paradox games
* Experience with other strategy games
* Tertiary skills in related fields such as UX, Art, or Sound

**Practical Information**
-------------------------


Please make sure you include a cover letter where you outline why you want to join Paradox, what level of experience with Paradox games you have, and what other games you play.


We would like your application to include any relevant work samples and/or a link to your portfolio if you have one. We are especially interested in examples of gameplay systems and related work that you have previously designed or implemented.


Join Paradox and help us shape Victoria 3! Design rich systems, create meaningful historical events, and build stories players will remember.

title: E-commerce Manager
description:
**E-commerce Manager**
----------------------

**Reports to: CEO**  

**Location: Stockholm, Sweden**

**Djerf Avenue is a community driven brand, founded on the belief that every product should be made with care, for the people who wear it, the people who make it, and the world we share. Built together with our community and partners, we create thoughtfully designed lifestyle products with a strong focus on ethical production and size inclusivity.**

**We are looking for an E-commerce Manager to take ownership of our online store and ensure a seamless, inspiring, and inclusive shopping experience for our community. This is a hands-on role where you will manage, optimize, and grow Djerf Avenue’s e-commerce presence, directly impacting how our products reach and inspire our community.**

 **The Role**

**You’re someone who thrives on ownership, structure, and execution. You will manage daily e-commerce operations, ensuring products, content, and processes are accurate, efficient, and customer-focused. You will collaborate closely with Production, Customer Service, Creative teams, and external partners such as our web development agency to enhance the online experience and support brand growth.**

 **What You’ll Do**

* **Manage product data, including pricing, categorization, and availability, while ensuring accurate website content and a consistent brand voice.**
* **Optimize the operations flow in the tech stack to make it more efficient.**
* **Handle shipping settings, costs, and regions in coordination with administration.**
* **Oversee daily e-commerce operations to maintain high-quality standards, troubleshoot and resolve issues, optimize the online experience, and drive sales through effective content, navigation, and campaigns.**
* **Maximize the value of current platforms and tools, and assist in implementing new systems to enhance the website.**
* **Ensure smooth collaboration with internal teams and external partners through proactive communication.**
* **Analyze key performance metrics and generate insights to inform strategic decisions, including AB testing, conversion optimization, and traffic growth.**
* **Ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements related to e-commerce operations.**
* **Lead initiatives to increase conversion, traffic, and average order value.**

 **About You**

* **Experience within e-commerce and customer experience, including customer acquisition and conversion rate optimization.**
* **Hands-on, pragmatic, and detail-oriented with a solution-focused mindset.**
* **Excellent verbal and written communication skills in both Swedish and English (additional languages a plus).**
* **Comfortable managing multiple tasks, working independently, and collaborating across teams.**
* **Passionate about creating an outstanding online customer experience and continuously improving processes.**
* **Able to analyze data and turn insights into measurable business value.**

 **Qualifications**

* **Minimum 3 years of work experience in e-commerce.**
* **Education in e-commerce or related fields.**
* **Experience with e-commerce platforms, CMS tools, analytics, and online merchandising tools.**
* **Strong understanding of online customer journeys, UX, and drivers of conversion and traffic.**
* **Ability to quickly learn and master new systems and tools.**

 **Start Date**

**We’re looking to fill this position as soon as possible. Selection and interviews are ongoing, and the position may be filled before the application deadline.**

**Please apply with your CV. Applications via email will not be accepted. We look forward to welcoming our next E-commerce Manager.**

**Location: This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden. You must have the legal right to work in Sweden.**

**We look forward to welcoming our next E-commerce Manager at Djerf Avenue!**

title: Head of Design
description:
Do you want to lead the design of Sweden’s most loved financial platform? At Avanza, we don't just "do" design – we use it to democratize finance.


We are looking for a Head of Design to lead our talented team in making the complex simple and the future of savings intuitive. We believe design creates clarity and control. You’ll lead the design of experiences that help millions shape a better future and make smarter financial decisions.


If you want to combine a deep love for the craft with bold, modern leadership in a culture that thrives on innovation and heart, this is where you belong.

**About the Role**  

As Head of Design, you masterfully balance a genuine passion for the craft with a sharp strategic mindset. You lead the vision for the entire design organization, from deep user insights to the final visual experience, and play a pivotal role in shaping both product strategy and business-critical decisions.


A central part of your mission will also be shaping design in the AI era, driving how we integrate AI and personalization to build a proactive customer experience rooted in security and trust. You help customers take control of their finances by translating complex technology and regulatory requirements into simple, intuitive experiences.


In this role, you will:

* Partner closely in the Product- and Tech leadership team, to ensure that delivering true customer value remains at the heart of everything we build.
* Scale and evolve our design organization, fostering a world-class creative and safe culture that attracts and develops top talent.
* Navigate organizational, technical, and regulatory complexity to drive alignment and synergy between teams and leadership.
* Drive innovation and strategic change with confidence, while hands-on and through your team ensuring a product where world-class UX makes the complex simple.


The Team: You report to our CPO and have the overall responsibility for a design organization of approximately 30 people. You directly lead three Design Leads, each responsible for teams of UX designers and UI specialists across different areas. Your mission is to coach them to success and ensure a cohesive, world-class customer experience across the entire product. You also hold a Design Lead role within the Customer Experience area, driving several key aspects of the overall experience.

**Who You Are**  

We are seeking a senior leader with world-class UX expertise, passionate about developing others and with a proven track record of coaching managers in fast-paced tech environments. You have a natural ability to zoom out and see the big picture, setting long-term direction, while also possessing a keen sense of how UI details and UX insights work together to create products that customers love and that strengthen both brand and trust.


An excellent designer in your own right, equally comfortable working hands-on and guiding your team when needed. We also believe that you have:

* Proven leadership experience from a similar role, ideally within complex digital products or platforms.
* A strategic and bold mindset, comfortable taking calculated risks while always keeping customer trust at the center.
* Strong relational skills, with a supportive and down-to-earth approach that naturally builds trust across an organization.
* A good understanding of the Swedish language. Since we primarily operate in Swedish, you need to be comfortable following and participating in discussions, though full spoken fluency is not a requirement.

**Application**  

Does this sound like you? Great! Send in your application by clicking the "Apply Now"/ "Skicka ansökan" button in the ad as soon as possible. We look forward to hearing from you!

*We kindly but firmly decline any contact from recruitment agencies or advertising sales representatives.*
company_description: Med visionen om att skapa en bättre framtid för miljoner människor går vi till jobbet för att skapa billigare, bättre och enklare produkter och tjänster för våra kunder!

title: UX Lead that turns curiosity into pixel-perfect experiences
description:
As our UX Lead, you won’t just “own the UX”. You’ll actively shape how millions of people experience Prisjakt, every single day. This is where empathy meets sharp thinking, and where your work moves fluidly between sketching flows, reviewing designs, and influencing product direction. If you get a kick out of turning messy insights into elegant solutions (and still care a bit too much about button states), keep reading.


You’ll lead our designers and researchers but this isn’t a step-away-from-the-craft kind of role. You’ll stay hands-on, designing, refining, and jumping into the details when and if it matters. Think of it as equal parts doing, guiding, and pushing things in the right direction. From AI-driven features to everyday price comparisons, you’ll help create experiences that feel thoughtful, useful, and just a little bit delightful. And you’ll work closely with Product, Engineering, AI, and Marketing, making UX a natural part of every decision, not an afterthought.

###### **Here’s what you’ll spend your time on:**

* Drive and shape the UX direction across products—while actively contributing to the design work yourself
* Support and mentor designers and researchers, giving direction without letting go of the craft
* Design and refine experiences across web, mobile, and AI-powered features
* Strengthen our design system and ways of working so great UX doesn’t happen by accident
* Bring user insights into everyday decisions, from quick iterations to bigger product bets

###### **We think you’ll thrive here if you:**

* Have 5+ years in UX or Product Design in fast-paced environments, and some experience stepping into a team lead role
* Have helped grow or shape design teams—and still enjoy getting your hands dirty in the work
* Naturally switch between big-picture thinking and hands-on design without losing momentum in either
* Strong portfolio that highlights strategic thinking, team impact, and high-quality design outcomes
* Standardize and evolve design systems, processes, and research practices
* Care deeply about users, accessibility, and getting the details right (yes, spacing still matters)
* Have a solid understanding of web, mobile, and ideally AI-enhanced experiences
* Turn research and data into design decisions that actually ship
* Explain your thinking in a way that makes both designers and non-designers go “ah, makes sense”
* Fluent in English and Swedish is needed and another Nordic language is a nice bonus.


At Prisjakt, we believe great experiences come from honesty, curiosity, and care. You’ll join a creative and collaborative environment where UX has a real voice and where your work will genuinely shape how people make smarter buying decisions. We work in a hybrid setup, meeting three times a week in Malmö, with flexible hours to make life fit. You’ll also get two paid hours every week to move your body, and your birthday off—no questions asked. If you want to know more of our dailies - read here Prisjakt and Culture

title: UX Designer — Design experiences that help people choose smarter, every day
description:
At Prisjakt, we help millions of people make smarter buying decisions. Great decisions start with great experiences and that’s where you come in.


We’re looking for a UX Designer who combines strong craft, curiosity, and a user-first mindset to help us improve and evolve our product experiences and who’s excited to keep growing along the way.

###### **Your mission (should you choose to accept it)**


As a UX Designer, you’ll contribute to creating intuitive, accessible, and well-crafted experiences across our web and mobile platforms. You’ll work closely with designers, engineers, and product managers to explore problems, test ideas, and bring solutions to life.


You care about both the big picture and the details. You’re curious, empathetic, and genuinely user-focused, using research and data to inform your decisions. You collaborate openly, value feedback, and bring a team-first mindset to your work.


You bring structure where it’s needed, but stay flexible as things evolve. You’re proactive and motivated to learn, improve, and gradually take on more responsibility to grow your skills and impact over time.

###### **What you’ll be doing**

* Contribute to UX design across product initiatives, from discovery to delivery
* Help translate user needs and business goals into thoughtful design solutions
* Support and participate in research (usability testing, interviews, competitor analysis)
* Use insights and data to inform design decisions
* Design flows, wireframes, and high-fidelity UI
* Contribute to and follow our design system to ensure consistency
* Collaborate closely with product, tech, and design peers
* Share feedback and continuously grow your skills and impact

###### **What you’ll bring**

* Around 3 years of experience designing digital products
* A portfolio that shows solid UX thinking, interaction design, and attention to detail
* Hands-on experience with prototyping and user research methods
* Experience working in cross-functional product teams
* Fluent in English and Swedish. Another Nordic language is a nice bonus.
* It’s a plus if you also bring:
* Experience in e-commerce or comparison services
* Experience designing for mobile-first or native apps
* Knowledge of accessibility standards (like WCAG)
* Fluent in English and Swedish is needed and another Nordic language is a nice bonus.


At Prisjakt, we believe great experiences come from honesty, curiosity, and care. You’ll join a creative and collaborative environment where UX has a real voice and where your work will genuinely shape how people make smarter buying decisions. We work in a hybrid setup, meeting three times a week in Malmö, with flexible hours to make life fit. You’ll also get two paid hours every week to move your body, and your birthday off—no questions asked. If you want to know more of our dailies - read here Prisjakt and Culture

title: Graphic Designer (fixed-term)
description:
**YOUR MISSION AT VOI**


We’re looking for a creative and detail‑oriented **Graphic Designer** to join our Tender team on a 12‑month fixed‑term contract. You’ll support our Senior Designer and the Commercial team by creating clear, engaging visual communication across print and digital – this is a graphic design role, not UX.


You’ll help produce high‑quality assets for both internal and external use, from tenders and presentations to marketing campaigns and social media content. This will include:

* Owning parts of the design and layout of tender documents and commercial proposals, ensuring clarity, quality and visual impact.
* Creating and refining Google Slides presentations in line with brand guidelines and commercial objectives.
* Developing and delivering visual assets for marketing campaigns across online and offline channels.
* Producing high‑quality print and digital materials (e.g. stickers, hangers, brochures, social media graphics) with a strong focus on consistency and craft.
* Collaborating closely with the Commercial team to ensure all materials are visually aligned with the brand identity and support business goals.
* Contributing ideas and constructive feedback in creative sessions and team discussions.
* Taking ownership of day‑to‑day graphic design initiatives, supporting team priorities and suggesting improvements.

**WHAT YOU NEED TO EMBARK**


You are a collaborative designer with strong visual skills, a structured way of working and an interest in clear, accessible communication. You enjoy working with others, managing several projects at once, and delivering well‑crafted work.

* Strong proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (InDesign, Photoshop, Illustrator,), Figma and Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides).
* Solid understanding of graphic design principles, layout, typography and colour theory.
* A portfolio showcasing your work in branding, marketing or visual design (longer‑form documents and/or presentations is a plus).
* Ability to manage multiple deadlines with strong attention to detail.
* A collaborative, proactive mindset and eagerness to learn and grow.


Don’t meet every requirement? If you are excited about this role and our mission, and have relevant, transferable skills, we encourage you to apply – even if you don’t check every box.

**WHY VOI?**


Working at Voi is more than just a job; our People Promise includes a personal voyage where you will grow as a professional and be a part of a team and culture that builds something meaningful for society. In addition to this you’ll have the opportunity to:

* Join Europe’s #1 micromobility operator and one of the fastest growing scale‑ups.
* Enjoy unlimited free Voi rides and a dog‑friendly office.
* Work together with inspiring, motivated and supportive colleagues towards a common goal.
* Join the micromobility revolution and be a part of creating sustainable cities made for living, free from noise and pollution.

title: UX Artist
description:
As a UX artist at Embark, you will enable amazing user experiences for our gamers, experiences that they will remember and talk about for decades. Here you will work closely together with other talents to create everything from 2D elements, animations, menus, HUD, to living and inviting scenes with 3D components.  

  

If you like to spend time thinking about how players explore and experience games, and perhaps you also imagine a future where players can be part of creating their own unique experiences, then Embark might be your future home  

  

**Example of responsibilities**  

* Collaborate with a wide range of talents at Embark to deliver a great user experience
* Create aesthetically delightful visuals that enhances the gameplay experience
* Evaluating different software, techniques, engines and pipelines and help us get better and change how we work in the future

  

**We would love if you have*** A creative and curious mind
* A strong passion for art, design and games
* Excellent visual skillset and deep artistic understanding and knowledge
* Experience of following directions and deliver to quality from a visual, game play and technical perspective
* Professional English communication skills

  



At Embark we offer competitive salaries, passionate colleagues to share knowledge with and much more, but most of all we invite you to take part of a journey into the unknown, to build creative, surprising and beautiful experiences together.  

  

We welcome game makers of all sex, class, colour, age, gender identity, education, religion, opinion, culture, nation of origin, language, sexual orientation, shape, size, and ability.  

  

Did we leave anyone out? Well, we welcome you, too! We think that the gaming industry is made better when everyone has a seat at the table.  

  

Be yourself at Embark and make games while doing so. Please apply with confidence. We can’t wait to hear from you (in English)!

title: Digital Designer (UI / Visual)
description:
We are looking for a Digital Designer who combines strong visual craft with a modern, curious mindset. Someone who loves shaping digital experiences — and who confidently uses AI tools as part of their design workflow to explore ideas faster, prototype smarter, and raise the bar creatively.
   

  

 What you’ll do
   

  

 You’ll work across web, app, and digital product design — from early concepts to polished visual systems and interfaces. You’ll collaborate closely with strategists, developers, and fellow designers, turning insights and ideas into work that’s clear, bold, and effective.
   

  

 You’ll also
   

  

* Balance creativity with structure — ideas, systems, and execution
* Present and explain design decisions clearly, and iterate based on feedback
* Help evolve how we work with design, tools, and processes over time
* Use AI-assisted tools for concept exploration, rapid prototyping, visual generation, or workflow efficiency


**About You**
* You have 3–5+ years of experience in digital / UI / visual design
* You translate business goals and user needs into elegant, usable design
* You enjoy agency life: varied briefs, fast pace, and close collaboration
* You care deeply about typography, layout, color, and detail
* You’re highly skilled in Figma and modern digital design workflows
* You’ve used — or are actively exploring — AI tools in real design projects
* Bonus if you’ve worked with design systems, motion, or speak Swedish (not required)


**About Above**
 Above is a design agency built on curiosity, collaboration, and craft. We work to change behaviors for the better — helping brands grow by creating meaningful and effective experiences.
   

  

 You’ll join a multidisciplinary team where creativity is taken seriously, feedback is part of everyday work, and people genuinely care about doing great work together. We believe in trust, flexibility, and giving designers space to grow — both creatively and professionally.
   

  

 Practical details
   

  

* Please include CV + portfolio (projects showing how you work matter most — AI-assisted work is a plus)
* Location: Stockholm (hybrid)
* If you’re not in Stockholm yet but open to relocating — tell us!

title: Webbredaktör Arbetsförmedlingen
description:
*Vill du arbeta i gränslandet mellan teknik, innehåll och användarupplevelse? Har du ett strukturerat arbetssätt och ett starkt intresse för webbplattformar och tillgänglighet? Vi söker två erfarna webbkommunikatörer – en med huvudinriktning externa kanaler och en med huvudinriktning interna kanaler - som vill bli en del av våra team i en samhällsviktig verksamhet. Tillsammans får vi Sverige i arbete.*




**Enheternas uppdrag**




*Digital kommunikation*
 Vi är 15 medarbetare med ansvar för extern webb, nyhetsbrev, sociala medier och språkfrågor. Enheten förvaltar och utvecklar myndighetens digitala kommunikation och säkerställer tydligt språk och en sammanhållen användarupplevelse.
   

  





*Ledningskommunikation*
 Vi är 13 medarbetare med ansvar för ledningskommunikation, förändringskommunikation, intranät och ger kommunikationsstöd till verksamheten. Enheten har målgruppsansvar för chefer och medarbetare samt ansvarar för varumärke och grafisk profil.
   

  





**Beskrivning av tjänsterna**




 Vi söker två webbredaktörer.
 


* Webbredaktör externa kanaler har huvudsaklig inriktning på extern webb men är också en resurs för intranätet.
* Webbredaktör interna kanaler har huvudsaklig inriktning på intranät men är även en resurs för extern webb.



 Du arbetar i ett team med webbredaktörer med uppgiften att redigera, anpassa och publicera innehåll. Som webbredaktör säkerställer du att innehållet följer krav på tillgänglighet, klarspråk och SEO. Både tjänsterna ställer krav på kompetens att tillgänglighetsanpassa alla typer av dokument och innehåll på webbsidor. Du hanterar många kontaktytor i myndigheten och arbetar både med löpande förvaltning och utveckling i nära samarbete med IT. För att lyckas behöver du arbeta lika bra självständigt som i grupp. Du behöver också ha ett lösningsorienterat arbetssätt.
   

  





 I rollen ingår också att:
 


* Producera, inventera och utveckla innehåll för webben.
* Analysera och följa upp användarbeteenden, föreslå förbättringar och hantera verksamhetens och målgruppernas behov.
* Delta och leda webbrelaterade utvecklingsprojekt.
* Vara bollplank i myndigheten i frågor gällande tillgänglighet.



**Grundläggande krav för tjänsten** 



* Universitet- eller högskoleutbildning om 180hp/120p inom media, kommunikation eller erfarenhet som bedöms likvärdig.
* Minst tre års erfarenhet av att arbeta med webb och webbutveckling.
* Erfarenhet av arbete med digital tillgänglighet.
* Mycket goda färdigheter i tal och skrift på svenska.



**Övriga meriter för tjänsten** 



* Erfarenhet av arbete inom statliga myndigheter eller stora organisationer.
* Erfarenhet av sökmotorsoptimering.
* Erfarenhet av UX och design.



 Den statliga värdegrunden guidar oss i vårt dagliga arbete. Som anställd hos oss förväntas du arbeta rättvist, opartiskt och med hög integritet, samt bidra till att upprätthålla och stärka förtroendet för vår myndighet.  
   

 Mer om statliga värdegrunden
   

  





 Utöver detta har du personlig förmåga och fallenhet att arbeta i enlighet med Arbetsförmedlingens medarbetarkriterier.
   

  





 Som medarbetare på Arbetsförmedlingen förväntas du vara:
   

**Ansvarstagande** 
 – ta ansvar för ditt arbete, samarbeta och bidra till en god arbetsmiljö.
   

**Handlingskraftig** 
 – ta initiativ, bidra till förbättringar och hantera förändringar.
   

**Resultatfokuserad** 
 – planera, följa upp och bidra till verksamhetsnytta och resultat.
   

  





**Vårt erbjudande**




 Tjänsterna är tillsvidareanställningar på heltid som kan komma att inledas med sex månaders provanställning. Placeringsorten är Arbetsförmedlingens huvudkontor i Solna.
   

  

 Vi erbjuder dig möjligheten att arbeta på en arbetsplats där vi värdesätter balans mellan arbete och privatliv. Du får tillgång till ett generöst semesteravtal, flexibla arbetstider samt möjlighet att arbeta på distans upp till 49 procent på årsbasis om verksamheten så tillåter. Hos oss ingår även friskvård under arbetstid och ett förmånligt friskvårdsbidrag.
   

  

 Som anställd på Arbetsförmedlingen har du också tillgång till kompetensutveckling och karriärmöjligheter inom myndigheten. Genom vårt arbete bidrar vi till ett inkluderande och hållbart samhälle, där du får möjlighet att göra en verklig skillnad för individer och för samhället i stort.
   

  





 Mer om att jobba hos oss
   

  





**Ansökan**




 Låter det här som en tjänst för dig? Välkommen med din ansökan senast 2026-04-09
   

  





 Bifoga ditt CV där du specificerar årtal, månad och arbetsuppgifter för varje anställning, samt intyg som styrker kraven om utbildning och/eller arbetslivserfarenhet i din ansökan.
 
 Det är viktigt att du besvarar urvalsfrågorna detaljerat och sanningsenligt, för att vi ska få en tydlig bild av din erfarenhet. Om efterfrågad information inte framgår riskerar du bli bortsållad i urvalet. Du behöver inte skicka in ett personligt brev.
   

  





 Under rekryteringsprocessen kan urvalstester förekomma. Vi kommer att hålla dig informerad genom hela rekryteringsprocessen. Mer om vår rekryteringsprocess. 
   

  





 Har du skyddade personuppgifter kontaktar du den rekryteringspartner som finns namngiven i annonsen så får du hjälp med att skicka in din ansökan.
   

  





 Vi arbetar aktivt för att vara en myndighet fri från diskriminering. Det är viktigt för oss att all kompetens tas tillvara och vi välkomnar alla sökande. Vi ser positivt på om du har kunskaper i något av våra nationella minoritetsspråk.
 **Välkommen till oss – vi jobbar för ett Sverige i arbete! Här arbetar du på regeringens uppdrag för en väl fungerande arbetsmarknad. Målet är att korta tiden i arbetslöshet genom att matcha arbetssökande med arbetsgivare. Våra insatser riktas i första hand till dem som står längst ifrån arbetsmarknaden och till de arbetsgivare som är öppna för att anställa dem med störst behov av stöd. Vi motverkar felaktiga utbetalningar och brott mot välfärdssystemen så att våra skattemedel används effektivt till det de är till för. Vi samarbetar med många andra aktörer och tillsammans hittar vi lösningar som gör skillnad - för ett Sverige i arbete.**
 Mer om vår organisation och vårat uppdrag

title: Product Director
description:
****About Magma Math****
 Magma is one of the world's fastest-growing and most celebrated EdTech companies, transforming math education through the power of AI. Our platform turns handwritten solutions into digital insights, empowering teachers to focus on creativity and deeper learning - while giving them real-time visibility into student progress.
   

  

 The result? More inclusive, engaging, and effective math classrooms for all learners.
   

  

 But we're not just building better tools. We're tackling one of the biggest challenges in global education: math
   

  

 underperformance. With 1 in 5 students globally struggling in math - a subject that forms the backbone of so many career paths - we believe the opportunity to improve outcomes at scale is massive.
   

  

 We're already the go-to solution in thousands of schools - and we're just getting started. As we expand rapidly across the U.S. and Europe, we're building a team as ambitious as our mission: to help millions of students unlock their full potential in math.
   

  

****The Role****
 The Product & Tech team at Magma is divided into three core experiences: Teacher, Student and District. With a growing user base and plenty of problems to solve, we're hiring a
 **Product Director of Student Experience** 
 to own the student-facing part of our product and take full ownership of the space.
   

  

 You will define the vision and strategy for the student experience, manage stakeholders across the organization, and align priorities across teams. You are ultimately responsible for making sure every feature we ship moves the needle for learners.
   

  

 At the same time, you will act as Product Manager for one of the product units - working side-by-side with developers, QA and designers to plan, build and launch features. You will own a domain end-to-end - from figuring out what problem is worth solving, to shipping a solution, to making sure it actually creates value.
   

  

**Who You Are And What You Know**
* Have experience leading and growing product teams; you know how to build structure and set meaningful KPIs
* Be hands-on across the full product development cycle; user research, design, implementation, testing. This is not a pure project manager role where you are only coordinating
* Have strong technical understanding, enough to engage with engineers
* Be structured and always questioning the status quo - you plan ahead and look for better ways to work
* Be curious and eager to learn - and a team player who understands others' perspectives


 Your responsibilities will include:
   

  

* Owning the vision, strategy and roadmap for the Student Experience
* Leading a team of developers, QAs and designers as hands-on PM for one product unit, from discovery to implementation.
* Coordinating efforts across the product, tech, customer success and revenue teams
* Setting KPIs and long-term goal structures
* Mentoring and developing team members


**Requirements**
* Proven track record in product management, including leadership experience
* Experience leading people and teams
* Engineering degree (or equivalent) from top tier university
* Startup experience — you know what you are getting yourself into!
* Experience of fast paced and high performance environment


**Nice to have**
* Experience as software engineer, designer, product manager, data science
* Experience in edtech or education
* Portfolio of products you've built


**What We Offer**
* Build and ship high touch products with positive impact on millions of users
* Be part of driving change in an industry
* Fantastic colleagues in a fun environment with great opportunities to learn
* Competitive remuneration & benefit package
* Annual company trip
* An opportunity to work on a meaningful mission and have an exceptional impact on education, society and how children learn math across the world


 Start: As soon as possible
   

  

 Office location: Stora Nygatan 27, 11227 Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

 Our recruitment process (all in office):
   

  

* 1st interview with Hiring Manager and Head of People
* 2nd interview with Hiring Manager and Magma member
* 3rd interview with Founders and Magma member


 Note: We practice a 5 day in office policy, and we look forward to working together closely with you.

title: Game Engineer UI/UX
description:
At Embark, we want to empower everyone to create games!
   

  

 You will be working on THE FINALS or ARC Raiders, alongside some of the most talented people in the games industry.
   

  

 As a Game Engineer UI/UX you will have a creative role, working together with designers, other engineers, artists, and user experience (UX) researchers to improve and develop new features for our game. Here you will have the opportunity to take responsibility for our game’s user interface and player experience.
   

  

 We are looking for someone who will help improve our UI framework and components with a special focus on performance, tooling and scalability.
   

  

 The game we’re making is built in Unreal, using C++, blueprints and a scripting language called Angelscript.
   

  

**Example Of Responsibilities**
* Implement C++ UI core technology in Unreal (e.g. Slate)
* Implement UI features in Unreal Engine using a mix of C++, Angelscript and Unreal Blueprints
* Collaborate with a wide range of talents at Embark to deliver a great user experience, better tools and a performant and top of the line UI


**We would love if you have**
* A creative and curious mind
* 5+ years of experience working within the game industry
* A strong background in C++
* Knowledge of using Unreal Engine with Slate/UMG
* An interest in computer science and programming as a craft
* Professional English communication skills.


 At Embark we offer competitive salaries, passionate colleagues to share knowledge with and much more, but most of all we invite you to take part of a journey into the unknown, to build creative, surprising and beautiful experiences together.
   

  

 We welcome game makers of all sex, class, colour, age, gender identity, education, religion, opinion, culture, nation of origin, language, sexual orientation, shape, size, and ability.
   

  

 Did we leave anyone out? Well, we welcome you, too! We think that the gaming industry is made better when everyone has a seat at the table.
   

  

 Be yourself at Embark and make games while doing so. Please apply with confidence. We can’t wait to hear from you (in English)!

* Previous roles as software engineer or data science
* Portfolio of products you've built


 What we offer
   

  

* Real ownership - you set the strategy, not just the slide deck
* Build and ship high touch products with positive impact on millions of users
* Be part of driving change in an industry
* Ambitious colleagues in a fun environment
* Competitive remuneration & benefit package
* Annual company trip
* An opportunity to work on a meaningful mission and have an exceptional impact on education, society and how children learn math across the world
